etymology.description: 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") = intertwined threads -> silk; thread
etymology.interpretation: In 丝(絲) (sī, "silk; thread"), the repetition of 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") signifies multiple threads intertwined, emphasizing the nature of silk or thread as consisting of numerous thin threads combined together.
etymology.simplification: 絲(絲) was simplified to 丝(絲) by streamlining the duplication of 糸(糸) to a simpler form.

examples.chinese: 她穿了一条丝裙(她穿了一條絲裙)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chuānle yì tiáo sī qún.

examples.chinese: 这条丝巾是从意大利买的(這條絲巾是從意大利買的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo sī jīn shì cóng Yìdàlì mǎi de.
examples.english: This silk scarf was bought in Italy.

examples.chinese: 舟子的汗水如丝般滑落(舟子的汗水如絲般滑落)。
examples.pinyin: Zhōuzǐ de hànshuǐ rú sī bān huáluò.
examples.english: The boatman's sweat slid down like silk.
